_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 (=100), currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase over that baseline. This headline rise reflects uneven price movement among components and a relatively recent acceleration. The index consolidates various commodity prices into one measure to track overall changes in typical breakfast-related soft commodities.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015 with a 99% increase, making it the largest contributor to the basket's rise. Orange juice follows with a 34% gain, while wheat and sugar have more modest increases of 19% and 13% respectively. These price shifts primarily result from weather events and diseases impacting specific crops, such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice, and frost or drought in arabica coffee-growing regions. As soft commodities trade on relatively thin markets, such supply shocks cause pronounced volatility rather than reflecting broad-based inflation.
notes.why: The headline price rise is driven by a few weather-sensitive, disease-affected soft commodities rather than systemic inflationary pressures. Because production risks remain elevated, prices for affected crops can stay high for multiple years until supply stabilizes. This underlines the importance of monitoring crop-specific conditions in these thin global markets.
